visual_notes: Madagascar celestite geodes — hollow limestone nodules lined with pale-blue tabular crystals — are among the most widely sold mineral specimens globally. The celestial blue color is gentle and consistent. Crystals often have a translucent quality with pearly luster on cleavage surfaces.
prompt_fragments: Madagascar celestite geode — hollow limestone cave of sky-blue tabular crystals
pale celestial-blue orthorhombic clusters — heaven's palette in strontium sulfate
pearly-blue celestite tabular crystal with pearlescent cleavage
vibe_notes: Celestite holds the color of open sky inside a limestone cave. The name is fitting — pale blue, pearly, translucent, quiet. Madagascar geodes are small sky-caverns. A gentle mineral with a celestial aspiration.
folklore_summary: The name 'celestite' or 'celestine' comes from Latin 'caelestis' (heavenly) — a reference to the pale sky-blue color. The element strontium was first isolated from celestite and strontianite found near Strontian, a village in Argyll, Scotland — giving the element its name. Madagascar's Sakoany celes
